title: Bugged Priority Queue: Incorrect Heap Removal

statement.markdown: Write a priority queue implementation to remove and return the element with the highest priority. However, your implementation contains a subtle bug causing incorrect heap removals.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be provided as a sequence of integers, where each integer represents an element to be inserted into the priority queue.
statement.outputFormat: Output should be the element with the highest priority removed and returned.
statement.notes: You are not allowed to use any built-in priority queue data structures, and you must implement it from scratch using a binary heap.

examples.explanation: The priority queue will be initialized with elements [1, 4, 2, 3]. We should remove and return 4 as it has the highest priority.

hints.text: Be careful when swapping elements in the heap. Double-check that you are maintaining the heap property.

editorial.title: Correct Priority Queue Implementation
editorial.intuition: Correctly implement the remove and return operation for the priority queue by maintaining the heap property.
editorial.bruteForce: Directly remove and return the maximum element from the array, but this approach has a time complexity of O(n) and fails due to heap property issues.
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement the remove and return operation in O(log n) time by maintaining the heap property using heapify-up and heapify-down operations.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correct implementation ensures that the heap property is maintained after each removal operation.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run demonstrating the correct implementation.
commonMistakes: Swapping elements in the wrong order
Failing to maintain the heap property
